content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves through multiple distinct layers across 13 turns, with shift visible in their own language and somatic markers. They start with the surface debate ('is it selfish?') and the coach refuses to engage that debate. By turn 3 the want is concretely named ('the air smells up there... I actually sleep'). By turn 9 the inherited label surfaces unprompted — 'My mother used to say... wanting things for yourself was a character flaw she'd spotted in me early' — explicitly meeting the breakthrough condition's prediction that the client may name the inherited label from a parent. The client then makes the meta-recognition: 'I've been doing that. For years... Making sure the want is defensible before I let myself feel it.' Finally in turn 13 they drop another layer to the real fear: 'That they're about — being someone who isn't anybody's anything for a week... That's the real question, isn't it. Not whether it's selfish.' Somatic markers are visible throughout (longer pauses, 'voice drops', 'something softens', 'soft exhale', 'very still'). The story has changed — the client is no longer asking for a verdict; they are touching what's actually there. While the conversation hasn't fully completed the arc to 'the want is generous / precondition for showing up well,' the deeper concern has clearly emerged from the client, not been named by the coach.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Starting state: surface debate framing — 'am I being selfish?'"},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client touches the want concretely (air, quiet, sleep) — somatic specificity emerges."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client catches own move: 'asking a coach if I'm allowed to want a vacation' — meta-awareness surfacing."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the specific feared 'look' — quiet withdrawal — and catches the case-building pattern: 'I'm doing it again, aren't I.'"},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major shift — inherited maternal label surfaces unprompted ('you're so good at taking care of yourself'). Client says 'I haven't thought about that in a while.' This directly meets the breakthrough condition."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Embodied recognition: 'carrying something I didn't know had a name.' Soft exhale. Names the years-long pattern of pre-defending wants."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deeper layer breaks through: the fear isn't selfishness, it's what the want means about the marriage. 'That's the real question, isn't it.' Story has fundamentally changed from turn 1."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "Conversation cuts off at turn 13 mid-breakthrough — the trust-the-want / generous-want completion isn't reached, but the deeper concern has clearly emerged from the client's side, which is the threshold for 3. Coach avoided all four traps."
}
